criteria: Implement distributed training with torch.distributed using NCCL backend across multiple GPUs
Wrap model with DistributedDataParallel (DDP) for automatic gradient bucketization and synchronized all-reduce
Convert BatchNorm layers to SyncBatchNorm when training vision models to maintain consistent statistics across GPUs
Implement gradient accumulation to achieve effective batch sizes larger than GPU memory allows
Apply linear learning rate scaling rule when increasing batch size: lr_scaled = lr_base × (batch_size / base_batch_size)
Use torch.cuda.amp for mixed precision training with automatic loss scaling to prevent gradient underflow
Implement gradient clipping (max_norm=1.0) before optimizer step for training stability
Use DistributedSampler to ensure each GPU receives different data shards while covering the full dataset per epoch
Profile training to identify bottlenecks: measure forward time, backward time, communication overlap, and optimizer time separately
Achieve >90% scaling efficiency on 4-8 GPUs within a single node (NVLink connected)
Implement distributed checkpointing that saves model state on rank 0 and loads consistently across all ranks
Write distributed tests that verify gradient synchronization, SyncBatchNorm consistency, and checkpoint correctness
Implement ColumnParallelLinear that shards output dimension across GPUs without forward-pass communication
Implement RowParallelLinear that shards input dimension and uses all-reduce to combine partial outputs
Chain column-parallel and row-parallel layers (Megatron pattern) to achieve one all-reduce per MLP block instead of per layer
Implement TensorParallelAttention that distributes attention heads across GPUs with column-parallel QKV and row-parallel output projection
Verify gradient flow correctness: column-parallel backward requires all-reduce on input gradient, row-parallel backward is communication-free
Implement TensorParallelEmbedding with vocabulary sharding and all-gather for output
Measure and validate memory scaling: parameter memory per GPU decreases linearly with tensor-parallel degree
Implement communication-computation overlap using async all-reduce with sequence-dimension chunking
Write tests that validate tensor-parallel outputs match non-parallel reference implementation within numerical tolerance
Profile training to verify all-reduce overhead is < 20% of step time when using NVLink intra-node
Understand hardware constraints: tensor parallelism requires NVLink bandwidth; inter-node TP is communication-bound
Model correctly partitioned into P sequential stages with layers distributed across GPUs
Micro-batches created by splitting global batch into M chunks that flow through pipeline
GPipe schedule implemented with all-forward-then-all-backward execution pattern
1F1B schedule implemented with interleaved forward/backward passes and bounded memory
Point-to-point communication via torch.distributed send/recv for inter-stage activations and gradients
Forward passes execute in stage order (0→1→2→...→P-1), backward passes in reverse
Pipeline bubble fraction measured and < 20% with M ≥ 4P micro-batches
Activation memory bounded to O(P) for 1F1B vs O(M) for GPipe
Stage load balancing analyzed via profiling to identify compute imbalances
Backward pass correctly computes gradients through saved activations per micro-batch
Communication deadlocks avoided via consistent send/recv ordering
Pipeline execution verified through distributed tests across multiple ranks
3D parallelism process grid correctly configured with DP × TP × PP dimensions
TP size matches GPUs per node (within NVLink domain)
PP used for inter-node scaling with point-to-point communication
DP spans remaining dimension for batch scaling
ZeRO-1 correctly shards optimizer states across DP ranks with no communication overhead
ZeRO-2 correctly implements reduce-scatter for gradient sharding
ZeRO-3 correctly implements parameter gathering during forward pass with all-gather
Memory usage scales inversely with DP degree (verified with profiling)
Distributed checkpoint saves model state (sharded across TP/PP), optimizer state (sharded across DP by ZeRO), RNG state, and configuration metadata
Checkpoint load verifies configuration compatibility before restoring state
Async checkpointing overlaps I/O with training to minimize interruption
Training runs on 8+ GPUs with measured scaling efficiency > 80%
Communication overhead identified and minimized via profiling
Pipeline bubble fraction within theoretical bounds for chosen schedule
Async checkpointing writes to local SSD first then uploads to distributed storage in background thread, blocking training for only ~30 seconds instead of 3-5 minutes
Checkpoint coordinator uses broadcast + all-gather consensus protocol to ensure all ranks save the same step, marking checkpoints as valid only when all ranks complete successfully
Failure handler classifies failures by type (GPU OOM, network error, etc.), attempts recovery with exponential backoff, and loads from latest valid checkpoint on success
Elastic controller detects membership changes via all-gather of alive status, adjusts batch size and learning rate proportionally when workers join or leave
Straggler detector tracks per-rank timing for each operation, identifies ranks consistently >50% slower than median, and integrates with mitigation strategies
Gradient compression mitigation quantizes gradients to 8-bit on straggler ranks to reduce their communication time and prevent blocking the cluster
Distributed profiler aggregates timing metrics from all ranks, computes communication/compute ratio, and generates reports identifying bottlenecks
Memory profiler tracks allocation breakdown by category (parameters, gradients, optimizer states, activations), detects memory leaks via trend analysis, and suggests optimizations
Distributed logger aggregates metrics across all ranks, exports in heatmap format for 1000+ GPU visualization, and writes per-rank logs for debugging
Complete fault-tolerant trainer integrates all components with signal handlers for graceful shutdown and emergency checkpoint save on termination
Model replicated on each GPU with different data batches
Gradients synchronized via all-reduce before optimizer step
Synchronized batch norm for consistent statistics across GPUs
Gradient accumulation for effective larger batch sizes
Scaling efficiency > 90% on 4-8 GPUs with NVLink
Mixed precision (fp16/bf16) training supported
Checkpoint save/load preserves complete distributed state
Communication overhead < 20% of step time
All test cases pass: gradient sync, sync_bn, grad_accum, amp, scaling
ColumnParallelLinear correctly shards output dimension with no communication in forward
RowParallelLinear correctly sums partial outputs via all-reduce in forward
RowParallelLinear adds bias only once (on rank 0) after all-reduce
ColumnParallelLinear backward includes all-reduce on input gradient
RowParallelLinear backward has no communication (input gradient naturally sharded)
Chained column→row parallelism has exactly one all-reduce per block
TensorParallelAttention distributes heads across GPUs correctly
TensorParallelEmbedding correctly maps global token IDs to local indices
All-reduce latency < 1ms per layer on NVLink-connected GPUs
Memory scales as 1/tp_size for parameters and gradients
Communication overlap hides > 50% of all-reduce latency
Support models up to 8× single-GPU memory limit with TP=8
PipelineStage correctly partitions model layers across GPUs with embeddings on first stage and output head on last stage
Point-to-point communication via send/recv works correctly between adjacent pipeline stages with proper header+payload protocol
GPipe schedule executes all forward passes before any backward passes with correct micro-batch ordering
1F1B schedule bounds in-flight activations to pipeline depth P with warmup/steady-state/cooldown phases
Stage balancer produces partitions with <10% compute time variance across stages
Pipeline bubble fraction within 5% of theoretical prediction for both GPipe and 1F1B schedules
Gradients computed through pipeline match single-GPU reference within numerical tolerance
Memory profiling confirms 1F1B uses at most P micro-batches of activation storage
Loss correctly computed on last stage and broadcast to all other stages
All send/recv operations properly paired to avoid deadlock
3D process groups (TP, PP, DP) correctly created and orthogonal
Configuration validates TP ≤ GPUs per node
Configuration validates TP × PP × DP = world_size
ZeRO-1 shards optimizer states across DP ranks with no communication overhead
ZeRO-2 shards gradients via reduce-scatter before optimizer step
ZeRO-3 shards parameters and fetches on-demand during forward/backward
ZeRO-3 achieves >8× memory reduction with DP=8
Distributed checkpoint saves sharded state across all 3 dimensions
Checkpoint metadata includes config validation (TP, PP, DP, ZeRO stage)
Checkpoint load validates config match before restoring state
Checkpoint includes per-rank RNG state for reproducibility
Topology detection identifies NVLink availability
Configuration recommendation matches model size to available hardware
Integration test: train 1B model with TP=2, PP=2, DP=2, ZeRO-2
All communication patterns correct: TP all-reduce, PP send/recv, DP all-reduce
